untranslatable_reason: Signal requires ranking stocks by 3-year historical volatility and selecting decile/quintile buckets (portfolio construction across universe), which requires cross-sectional ranking logic not supported by single-instrument indicator conditions. hvol_20 is only 20-day volatility. The 1095-day lookback and minimum variance portfolio approach cannot be expressed as simple indicator threshold rules on individual securities.
translation_notes: This signal describes a cross-sectional ranking and portfolio weighting strategy (minimum variance portfolio construction) rather than a time-series entry/exit signal. A proper implementation would require: (1) calculating 3-year rolling volatility for all stocks in universe, (2) ranking and bucketing by volatility deciles/quintiles, (3) constructing long/short portfolios from extreme buckets, and (4) periodic rebalancing. Standard backtesting engines typically require per-instrument conditions, not universe-wide ranking and weighting logic.
